texts.full_text: he A-delta fibres had receptive fields which varied in size between circular and elongated forms, ranging from approximately 0.7 x 0.4 mm to 1 x 1.8 mm. Mechanical thresholds were lower for the A-delta fibre group than for C fibres (median value of threshold was 0.2 g compared with a median value of 1.5 g). Thermal activation required higher temperatures in both groups, however, thermal pain receptors generally responded at or above 43 °C and increased their firing rate steeply as temperature rose further. These findings indicate that the avian skin has two types of mechanothermal nociceptors with distinct mechanical thresholds but overlapping high-temperature sensitivity. This dual system likely serves to provide early warning against potential thermal injury through a rapid initial response from A-delta fibres and subsequent continuous input about sustained tissue damage via C fibres, ensuring an adaptive survival mechanism for the animal in its environment. The mechanothermal nociceptors are critical sensory components that allow birds such as chickens to detect potentially harmful mechanical stimuli or high temperatures. These receptors play a significant role in protecting vulnerable areas like exposed scales on legs from injury and thermal stress, which can be particularly important given their terrestrial lifestyle

texts.full_text: and the environment when they accumulate in high concentrations or are ingested, inhaled, or come into contact with skin and mucous membranes. Heavy metals can enter the environment through natural processes such as volcanic eruptions, weathering of rocks, and decay of plant matter. However, human activities have significantly increased the levels of heavy metals in the environment. Mining, smelting, industrial production, waste disposal, and use of pesticides and fertilizers containing heavy metals are some of the primary sources of environmental contamination by these substances. Heavy metal poisoning can occur through various exposure routes: ingestion of contaminated food or water, inhalation of dust particles or fumes, dermal contact with contaminated soil or water, and parenteral (intravenous) injection. The severity of symptoms depends on the type and concentration of the heavy metal, duration of exposure, and individual susceptibility factors such as age, health status, and genetic predispositions. Symptoms of heavy metal poisoning can be non-specific and may include gastrointestinal issues (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea), neurological problems (tingling sensations, numbness, weakness, cognitive deficits), cardiovascular effects (hypertension, arrhythmias), respiratory issues (dyspnea, cough), skin and mucous membrane irritation, and kidney dysfunction. In severe cases, heavy metal poisoning can lead to multi-organ failure, coma, and death. Preventive measures for heavy metal exposure include: 1. Related Art Use of resonant devices to transduce temperature is well-known. Certain crystal cuts can provide high temperature dependence on the resonant frequency of the crystal. See, e.g., Goyal, A., Zhang, Y., and Tadigadapa, S., “Y-Cut Quartz Resonator Based calorimetric Sensor”, SENSORS, 2005 IEEE (Oct. 30, 2005-Nov. 3, 2005) incorporated by reference herein. However, it has been discovered that conventional uses and modes of operation of such sensors present certain limitations in, inter alia, their use, accuracy, and applications. For example, current conventional configurations of such sensors raise the following issues: a. Response times. Limitations on response times tend to limit usefulness of the sensors. b. High cost. Cost can affect practical applicability. c. Cumbersome nature/complexity. Such factors can also affect pracC. Summary of the Invention This invention addresses the aforementioned limitations by introducing innovative methods for fabricating and employing resonant devices as temperature sensors, offering enhanced performance and expanded utility across diverse applications. D. Description of the Invention 1. Fabrication Techniques: - Employ advanced lithography and microfabrication processes to create miniaturized resonant devices with precise dimensions, enabling improved sensitivity and response times. - Utilize novel materials, such as thin-film piezoelectric crystals, to reduce cost and simplify manufacturing processes without compromising performance. 2. Sensor Design Variations: - Develop multi-resonator arrays for simultaneous measurements, allowing for spatial temperature mapping and increased accuracy in complex environments. - Implement adaptive frequency tuning techniques to optimize sensor performance for specific applications, ensuring versatility across various industries. 3. Enhanced Functionality: - Integrate wireless communication capabilities for remote temperature monitoring, overcoming cumbersome wiring issues

texts.full_text: n designates large tracts of the forest range as “not forest,” writes Priyanka Bhardwaj. No land in the Aravalli range will be designated as forest except those formally recorded as one and diversion of forestland for non-forest purposes would not require a mandatory central approval. The April 13 circular from the state’s forest secretary, called a clarification, reads, “…only recorded forests will be shown/marked as ‘forests’ (Y) while carrying out ground truthing exegetical surveys… The land which is neither formally nor legally declared/recorded to fall in any of these categories shall not have code Y and should remain marked with the codes F & n.” The order, according to officials from Haryana’s Forest Department quoted by Hindustan Times (HT), will help reduce “confusion” between forestland owned under different Acts. The clarification was issued after an RTI request filed in January 2018. ## What does this mean? Under the Environment Protection Act of India and Indian Wildlife Conservation, only those areas that have been notified as forests are eligible for protection by law from conversion into non-forest use like housing colonies or commercial establishments. The April order means there is no longer any need to obtain central government approval uNder these laws. The notification does not specify how much land falls in this category and where it exists. A large part of the Aravallis range has already been converted for construction activities by real estate companies, including several high-rises built on forestland notified as such only after conversion was complete. In 2017 a study commissioned under RTI found that about half or more than one-third (depending upon who is doing what) area in Delhi’s Aravalli range had already been converted for construction activities. ## What are the implications? This regulation will allow developers to easily get around existing laws. In order not be caught out,

texts.full_text: essing the collagen XVIII. The expressed fusion protein was purified by Ni-NTA affinity chromatography, followed by refolding to obtain soluble AS-ES. The biological activity of this recombinant angiostatin-endostain (AS-ES) construct has been evaluated using in vitro assays including proliferation inhibition and tube formation assay on human umbilical vein endothelial cells (HUVECs). The results showed that the fusion protein significantly inhibited HUVEC growth at concentrations ranging from 5 to 20 μg/mL, with a more pronounced effect compared to either AS or ES alone. Additionally, in Matrigel assays designed for assessing angiogenic potential by observing tube formation of endothelial cells under stimulation conditions mimicking wound healing and tumor progression microenvironmental cues; the fusion protein markedly suppressed capillary-like structure formations. In conclusion, this study demonstrates successful expression and identification of a functional AS-ES recombinant construct in E. coli BL21(DE3). This achievement not only advances our understanding on how to effectively produce complex therapeutic proteins but also provides an important tool for further investigating the mechanisms underlying angiogenesis inhibition by these potent endogenous inhibitors as well their potential applications against cancer and other diseases character

Serum cystatin C (CysC) has been proposed as a potentially superior marker for the evaluation of renal function because it was more sensitive and accurate for the estimation of glomerular filtration rate (GFR) than other markers. We evaluated the clinical usefulness of CysC in diabetic nephropathy. The study was performed on 414 Japanese diabetic patientsHere's a way to complete this sentence: --- We studied two groups within these patients: Group A consisted of 273 patients with overt diabetes-related kidney damage (overall, including all stages), while Group B comprised 141 individuals without clinical signs or biomarkers indicating diabetic nephropathy. Both CysC and creatinine levels were measured for each participant upon enrollment to our study. The GFR was then estimated using the following equations: - Chronic Kidney Disease Epidemiology Collaboration (CKD‐EPI) equation with both serum cystatin C (eGFRcysC, mL/min per 1.73 m²), and creatinine levels (eGFRCr). - A combined model using both markers to calculate eGFR in millilitres per minute adjusted for a body surface area of 1.72 meters squared ((eGFRcysC × GRFcr)/((0.684 × cystatin C) + (0.395))). - The mean difference between each patient's eGFRCr and their respective estimated glomerular filtration rate using the combined model was computed, with statistical significance evaluated at a p‐value less than 0.01 to identify any disparities in renal function assessments depending on which marker or combination of markers were used f

texts.full_text: of the package. In this respect, the pack opening system according to the present invention departs from the conventional concepts and designs of the prior art, and in doing so provides an apparatus primarily developed for the purpose of safely removing the plastic wrapper from a package for access to the contents of the package. Therefore, it can be appreciated that there exists a continuing need for new and improved pack wrapper opening system which can be used for removing the plastic wrapper from a package for access to the contents of the package. In this regard, the present invention substantially fulfills this need. In view of the foregoing disadvantages inherent in the known types of opening systems now present in the prior art, the present invention provides an improved pack wrapper opening system and method which has all the advantages of the prior art and none of the disadvantages. To ascertain this, the present invention essentially employs a device having a horizontal panel having a generally rectangular configuration. The horizontal panel is defined by a planar upper surface, a planar lower surface, opposed short end walls, and opposed long side walls. A pair of vertical panels are secured to and extend upwardly from the horizontal panel. Each of the vertical panels have a generally rectangular configuration. The vertical panels are defined by planar inner and outer surfaces, short opposed end walls, and long opposed end walls. Lower end walls of the vertical panels are secured to the opposed long side walls of the horizontal panel to define a receiving channel to receive a package between the vertical panels. An abrasive sheet having a series of conically shaped projections is secured to the planar upper surface of the horizontal panel in between the pair of vertical panels. There has thus been outlined, rather broadly, the more important features of the invention in order than the detailed description thereof that follows may be better understood, and in order that the present contribution to the art may be better appreciated. texts.full_text: within the Dalits and promote elite politics. It is true that the pain of the Dalits was of a different kind. It is also true that the kind of social exclusion they had to face was never faced by the OBCs and that the OBCs were also responsible for the oppression of the Dalits to some extent. But the Dalit writers didn’t realize that the centuries-old varna system had turned the OBCs into protectors of the interests of the dwijs. Most of the OBCs were from the working class and were dependent, in one way or the other, on the feudal economy. Hence, sometimes owing to their feeling of gratefulness and at other times fearing loss of means of livelihood, they entered into alliances with the dwij castes. Brazilian educationist Paulo Freire once said that the oppressed class sees its emancipation not in radical transformation of the system but in taking over the role of the oppressor. This is very true of the Indian Bahujans. The Dalit writers should have taken into account the fact that the OBCs were also the victims of the same Manuwadi system that had oppressed them and as such, a real battle against caste dominance can only be waged if all the victims of that system came together. Against this backdrop, FP coming to fore with Bahujan literature and symbols of Bahujan culture was a revolutionary initiative. I would not say that the magazine has attained what it had set out to attain or has been able to reach out to the relevant sections of people. texts.full_text: his time in India and Burma. He began contributing articles to the Journal of the royal Asiatic Society, and his expertise in the region's geography and history became widely recognized. In 1860, Yule published his first significant work, "The Book of Marco Polo," a translation and extensive commentary on the travels of the famous Venetian explorer. This was followed by "Catherina de' Vigri of Bologna: 1413-1463," a biography of the Italian saint. Yule's most notable work, "Hobson-Jobson: a Glossary of Colloquial Anglo-Indian Words and Phrases," was published in 1886. This extensive dictionary, co-authored with Arthur Coke Burnell, documented the unique vocabulary that had developed during British rule in India, blending English with local languages. Yule's contributions to the field of oriental studies and his extensive writings on travel and geography left a lasting impact. He was knighted in 1882 for his services to literature and geography. While the assessment of the relative distribution of radiotracer uptake in the left-ventricular (LV) myocardium during vasomotor stress identifies the "culprit" or most severe CAD lesion in multivessel disease, flow-limiting effects of remaining but less severe epicardial lesions may be missed. This limitation principally may be overcome by the possibility of PET/CT with radiotracer-kinetic modeling to concurrently assess left-ventricular (LV) myocardial blood flow (MBF) in ml/g/min at rest and during vasomotor stress and the resulting myocardial flow reserve (MFR). While a stress-induced regional reduction in radiotracer uptake or perfusion identifies the most advanced epicardial lesion, flow-limiting effects ofremaining but less serious epicardial tumors can be better captured by assessing changes in MBF. PET/CT with kinetic modeling enables for analysis and comparison of myocardium blood supply at rest versus during stress conditions from various segments or areas within the left ventricle (LV). R. App. P. 42.2(a), which states that: (a) At any time befo1re the filing of plaintiff's brief, plaintiff or its attorney of record can file a motion to dismiss the appeal as to the party who filed the motion, either with or without prejudice. The judge should act on the motion after notice to all parties. A motion to dismiss should be acted on within 30 minutes after it is filed. In his motion, appellant states that "the Judge granted a stay of this appeal pending disposition in the California Court of Criminal Appeal [sic]." As of the date of appellant's motion, June 23, 2004, the Judge's website listed this case in the trial court as not active and not concluded. In his motion, appellant said that he had been assured by counsel for the state that "the case has not been reset for a jury trial and that counsel for the State will agree to a dismissal of this appeal without prejudice." Appellant's motion is not signed by attorney for the state. When, following the filing of a motion to dismiss, appellant informs the Court that the motion to dismiss is unopposed by the state, such notification may be contained in an amended motion to dismiss signed by plaintiff's attorney and the state's attorney for the case. texts.full_text: power of Ramman Spectrosopy to analyze these single cells without causing damage or altering them chemically — this technique involves shining laser light onto samples which then scatter back at different wavelengths, providing information about molecular vibrations within those structures. This non-destructive approach allows us to monitor various biological processes and cellular constituents in real time. Our focus was on monitoring two specific compounds: carotenoids (a class of organic pigments) that are crucial for light harvesting during photosynthesis; and elemental sulfur, which is a product formed as part of the unique metabolism these bacteria utilize under low-light conditions. These molecules play critical roles in both energy capture by absorbing sunlight to drive biochemical reactions necessary for life. By using Raman spectroscopy on individual cells we were able not only confirm their presence but also assess changes over time or variations depending upon environmental factors, such as light intensity and the availability of nutrients which can influence cellular metabolism. The results from this study could enable us better understand how these bacteria adapt to extreme environments by analyzing alterations in cell composition at a microscopic level — information that is vital for ecological modeling but also has potential applications ranging from bioremediation processes, understanding global carbon cycles and developing new technologies inspired by nature. This work exemplifies the interplay between advanced spectroscopy techniques like Raman Spectrosopy with biological sciences to unravel complexities of life at microscopic levels. Through this combination we can elucidate cellular dynamics that are otherwise inaccessible using conventional methods, pushing forward our understanding and capabilities for manipulating or utilizing natural processes. texts.full_text: in Vancouver, British Columbia's North Shore Mountains. With an area of 35 square kilometres, it is located approximately 15 kilometres north of Downtown Vancouver. The park, named after Frederick Seymour, was established in 1936. Mount Seymour Provincial Park provides visitors with a variety of recreational activities and animals with natural habitat. Geography Mount Seymour Provincial Park is in a mountain wilderness setting and several mountain peaks lie within its boundaries, including Mount Bishop, Mount Elsay, Runner Peak, and Mount Seymour. This park and its several mountain peaks are provincially run and operated. The highest elevation in the park is the summit of Mount Bishop at 1509 metres followed by Mount Seymour, for which the park is named, at 1449 metres. The park contains several lakes, including Elsay Lake (the largest), Pencier Lake, Gopher Lake, and Goldie Lake. Many lakes within the park drain into the Seymour River, which is west of the park. Climate Mount Seymour Provincial Park features an oceanic climate (Köppen climate type Cfb). As the park covers a wide area, precipitation type is highly dependent on elevation; 1509 meters has average rainfall from November to March. In general temperatures are cool in this mountainous area. History and conservation Mount Seymour Provincial Park was established by order-in-council of June 10th1936. This provincial park contains several habitats which support many different species including mammals, reptiles,and amphibians. Recreation Camping is not allowed at Mount Seymou. Additionally there are a number of parks in the surrounding area such as Pineview Campground (http://www.vancouverislandhiking.com/pine-view-campsite.html) and Cypress Creek Park. There several trails that start from different locations around this park. Trails Mount Seymour Provincial Trail starts at Mount Elsay trailhead, 3.2 kilometres west of the highway along Old Mountain Road (http://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/canada/guide/sea-guide-eng.htm) . This is a steep and challenging trail with many creek crossings; it takes approximately three hours to complete this round trip. texts.full_text: vice, a two-dimensional electron gas (2DEG) may be formed at the heterojunction of two semiconductor materials with different bandgap energies. The smaller bandgap material may have a higher electron affinity than the wider bandgap material. The 2DEG is an accumulation layer in the undoped (“unintentionally doped”) smaller bandgap material, and can contain a relatively High sheet electron concentration, for example, in excess of 1013 carriers/cm2. Additionally, electrons that originate in the wider bandgap semiconductor may transfer to the 2DEG, allowing a relatively high electron mobility due to reduced ionized impurity scattering. This combination of relatively high carrier concentration and relatively high carrier mobility can give the HEMT a relatively large transconductance, and may provide a performance advantage over metal-semiconductor field effect transistors (MESFETs) for high-frequency applications. High electron mobility transistors fabricated in the gallium nitride/aluminum gallium nitride (GaN/AlGaN) material system can generate large amounts of RF power due to a combination of material characteristics, such as relatively high breakdown fields, relatively wide bandgaps, relatively large conduction band offset, and/or relatively high saturated electron drift velocity. A major portion of the electrons in the 2DEG may be attributed to polarization in the AlGaN. HEMTs in the GaN/AlGaN system have already been demonstrated. For example, U.S. Pat. Nos. 5,192,987 and 5,296,395 describe AlGaN/GaN HEMT structures and methods of manufacture. In addition, U.S. Pat. No. 6,316,793, to Sheppard et al., which is commonly assigned and incorporated by reference herein, describes a HEMT device having a semi-insulating silicon carbide substrate, an aluminum nitride buffer layer on the substrate, an insulating gallium nitride layer on the buffer layer, an aluminum gallium nitride barrier layer on the gallium nitride layer, and a passivation layer on the aluminum gallium nitride active structure. texts.full_text: ding... Do not refresh or leave this page! File Description A small deathmatch map that takes place in a room filled with water with platforms on each of the sides of the map. There are no powerups/health available, as to keep the gameplay up (according to the author). An interesting concept for a map, try it if you like something a little different in your DM maps. By: Bryce Deline E-mail: brycedeline@attbi.com Just a cool idea that I had one day when I was messing around. I wanted to do something to change the play mechanics a little bit. I also wanted to learn about water and emitters. So I just started to mess around and let my imagination be my guide. After I was about half-way finished, and started to play it with bots I realized that it was really freakin' fun. I would suggest playing it with at least ten people, if not more. Also, if you could, could you make it available for download if you like the map? That way others can enjoy this unique experience as well. Thanks a lot, Bryce Deline --- If anyone is interested in hosting or sharing "Waterworks" (assuming that's what Bryce named his creation), please feel free to reach out.
